name: Sovereign identity
description: Self-custodied credentials no central party can revoke

name: Portable attestations
description: Vouches and verifications that travel across platforms

name: Contextual disclosure
description: Share different facets of identity in different contexts without correlation

name: Trust graph portability
description: Relationships and reputations migrate when you move platforms

name: Proof of humanity
description: Prove you're a real person without surrendering personal data

name: End-to-end encrypted
description: Content readable only by intended recipients — not servers or providers

name: Protocol federation
description: Messages traverse platform boundaries without a central relay

name: Consent-based audiences
description: Explicit circle scoping — no algorithmic amplification

name: Ephemeral or archival
description: Different retention semantics for different communication contexts

name: Works offline / mesh
description: Communication works without reliable internet connectivity

name: Composable groups
description: Groups can nest, federate, and form sub-groups without losing sovereignty

name: Consent-based governance
description: Decisions are recorded, attributable, and revisable

name: Slow deliberation
description: Space for reflection before resolution — not just instant polling

name: Dissent pathways
description: Minority positions are preserved, not flattened by consensus pressure

name: Cross-instance federation
description: Groups on different platforms coordinate without data loss

name: Non-gamified tracking
description: Relationship history visible to participants — not to platform algorithms

name: Emotion-aware design
description: Prosocial patterns that support depth over performance

name: Anti-addiction architecture
description: No infinite scroll, no engagement optimization, intentional navigation

name: Restorative process tools
description: Structured support for naming tension and working through it

name: Collective field sensing
description: Mechanisms for groups to take stock of their relational health

name: Conflict-free editing
description: Multiple people edit without data loss — CRDTs handle merge automatically

name: Provenance tracking
description: Know where information came from and who attested to it

name: Version history
description: Knowledge base evolves but the full history is preserved

name: Interoperable formats
description: Documents portable across platforms without lock-in

name: Nested coordination
description: Local → regional → global without flattening local sovereignty

name: Decentralized scheduling
description: Event discovery without a central platform owning the graph

name: Ecosystem mapping
description: Dynamic maps of who's doing what, federated across instances

name: Civic integration
description: Structured pathways from community organizing to formal governance participation

name: Relational matching
description: Match needs to capacities through trust graph — not algorithm

name: Non-monetary exchange
description: Track commitments and gifts without requiring a financial ledger

name: Transparent stewardship
description: Community-owned assets with auditable governance

name: Private need expression
description: Express needs without exposure beyond chosen circles

name: Peer learning networks
description: Learn from others in decentralized, community-owned contexts

name: Open credentials
description: Portable verifiable credentials not locked to any single institution

name: Knowledge commons
description: Shared knowledge bases governed by contributors, not platforms

name: Self-hostable
description: Communities can run their own instances on their own infrastructure

name: Data portability
description: Full export/import of identity, content, and relationships

name: Local-first storage
description: Data lives on your device and syncs when connected — works offline

name: Privacy-preserving AI
description: Inference happens without sending community data to third-party servers

name: Gradual decentralization
description: On-ramps from centralized platforms without requiring technical sophistication

name: Geographic data federation
description: Location data shared across instances without a central map authority

name: Community mapping
description: Maps built and governed by the communities they represent

name: Local network routing
description: Route messages and data through local geographic networks
